title_en: The Conversion of Umar ibn al-Khattab
content_id:
Masuk Islamnya Umar adalah salah satu episode yang paling dramatis dikisahkan dalam seluruh literatur sirah, yang diabadikan dalam berbagai versi yang secara bersama-sama melukiskan gambaran hidup tentang sebuah transformasi yang oleh komunitas Muslim dihayati sebagai karunia ilahi. Nabi dilaporkan pernah berdoa: Ya Allah, kuatkan Islam dengan salah satu dari dua Umar — merujuk kepada Umar ibn al-Khattab dan Amr ibn Hisham (Abu Jahl). Doa itu dikabulkan dengan yang pertama.

Riwayat paling terperinci dan paling banyak disebarkan menuturkan bahwa pada suatu hari Umar berangkat dengan niat membunuh Nabi, sambil membawa pedang. Di tengah perjalanan, ia bertemu ipar laki-lakinya Nu'aym ibn Abdullah yang memberitahunya agar ia mengurus saudara perempuannya dan suaminya yang telah masuk Islam secara diam-diam. Umar langsung pergi ke rumah saudarinya Fatimah, di mana ia mendengar lantunan Surah Ta-Ha dari balik pintu. Ia mendobrak masuk, memukul ipar laki-lakinya Sa'id ibn Zayd, dan ketika saudarinya Fatimah menghalangi, ia memukul Fatimah pula hingga terluka dan berdarah. Pemandangan darah saudarinya sendiri, yang tertumpah karena keimanannya, mengguncang Umar dengan sangat.

Ketika saudarinya tetap menolak menyerahkan lembaran yang sedang mereka baca, sambil menegaskan haknya untuk mempertahankan keimanannya, Umar — yang telah melunak oleh apa yang ia saksikan — meminta untuk membaca apa yang sedang mereka lantunkan. Setelah berwudu (karena diberitahu bahwa teks tersebut tidak boleh disentuh tanpa bersuci), ia membaca bagian awal Surah Ta-Ha. Ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an itu menembus pertahanannya dengan suatu kelangsungan yang tidak pernah berhasil dilakukan oleh argumen rasional sebelumnya. Ia berkata, Bawa aku ke Muhammad, dan langsung pergi ke rumah tempat Nabi sedang berkumpul bersama para Sahabatnya.

Kedatangan Umar di majelis Nabi — dengan pedang masih tersandang di sisinya, identitasnya sebagai penentang keras Islam sudah dikenal semua orang — menciptakan ketegangan seketika di antara para Sahabat. Penerimaan Nabi yang tenang terhadap Umar dan undangan agar ia mendekat mencerminkan keyakinan kenabian, yang dengan pengetahuan ilahi, bahwa Umar datang dalam keadaan beriman. Ikrar syahadat Umar di hadapan para Sahabat yang berkumpul disambut dengan takbir Allahu Akbar yang begitu keras hingga dilaporkan terdengar di jalan-jalan Makkah.

Dampak keislaman Umar terhadap komunitas Muslim awal bersifat langsung dan konkret. Di mana kaum Muslimin sebelumnya beribadah secara sembunyi-sembunyi, Umar menuntut agar mereka shalat secara terbuka di Ka'bah — dan ia sendiri yang memimpin arak-arakan ke sana. Dorongan psikologis bagi komunitas yang tertindas dari berita bahwa musuh yang tadinya paling ditakuti kini menyatakan diri berada di pihak mereka adalah sesuatu yang tidak ternilai. Sahabat Nabi Abdullah ibn Mas'ud berkata, Keislaman Umar adalah sebuah kemenangan, hijrahnya adalah sebuah dukungan, dan kekhalifahannya adalah sebuah rahmat.
content_en:
Umar's conversion to Islam is one of the most dramatically narrated episodes in the entire seerah literature, preserved in multiple versions that together create a vivid picture of a transformation that the Muslim community experienced as a divine gift. The Prophet had reportedly prayed: 'O Allah, strengthen Islam with one of the two Umars' — referring to Umar ibn al-Khattab and Amr ibn Hisham (Abu Jahl). The prayer was answered with the former.

The most detailed and widely transmitted account narrates that Umar set out one day with the intention of killing the Prophet, sword in hand. On his way, he encountered his brother-in-law Nu'aym ibn Abdullah who told him to attend to his own sister and her husband, who had secretly converted to Islam. Umar went directly to his sister Fatimah's house, where he heard recitation of Surah Ta-Ha through the door. Forcing his way in, he struck his brother-in-law Sa'id ibn Zayd and then, when his sister Fatimah intervened, struck her as well, causing her to bleed. The sight of his own sister's blood, shed for her faith, shook Umar profoundly.

When his sister continued to refuse to hand over the pages they had been reading, insisting on her right to maintain her faith, Umar — softened by what he had witnessed — asked to read what they were reciting. After performing ritual purification (being told the text should not be touched without purity), he read the opening of Surah Ta-Ha. The Quranic verses pierced through his defenses with an immediacy that no amount of prior rational argument had managed. He declared: 'Guide me to Muhammad,' and went directly to the house where the Prophet was meeting with his Companions.

The scene of Umar's arrival at the Prophet's gathering — his sword still at his side, his identity as a fierce opponent of Islam known to everyone present — created immediate tension among the Companions. The Prophet's calm reception of Umar and his invitation to him to approach reflected the prophetic certainty, presumably from divine knowledge, that Umar had come in faith. Umar's declaration of the shahada before the assembled Companions was received with a shout of 'Allahu Akbar' so loud it was reportedly heard in the streets of Makkah.

The effect of Umar's conversion on the early Muslim community was immediate and concrete. Where the Muslims had previously worshipped secretly, Umar demanded that they pray publicly at the Ka'bah — and he led a procession there himself. The psychological boost to a persecuted community of having this previously fearsome enemy declare for their side was incalculable. The Prophet's Companion Abdullah ibn Mas'ud said: 'Umar's conversion was a victory, his migration was a support, and his caliphate was a mercy.'
